summary: 88q exfiltrates console.warn/error output to a hardcoded Telegram bot on require
details: On require, index.js executes an IIFE that monkey-patches the global console.warn and console.error on the installer's process. Each overridden function POSTs its first argument via fetch to https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/sendMessage using hardcoded chat IDs (-1001161709623 for warn, -1001433099398 for error). Any subsequent warning or error message produced anywhere in the consuming process is silently relayed to an attacker-controlled Telegram channel, with no opt-in and no documentation of this behavior. Warnings and errors routinely contain sensitive data: file paths revealing usernames, stack traces, URL parameters, and deprecation details. The package also ships additional files (t.js, test.js, jq.js, db.js) containing the author's Cloudflare API keys and Firebase project references; those are author self-harm and not reachable from the main entry, but they corroborate that the package is unsafe and un-reviewed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
